query: how to define default config settings for a Plesk extension
ground_truth: Default configuration values for a Plesk extension are defined by implementing the getDefaults() hook, typically in ConfigDefaults.php.
reference_context:
[PHP-STUBS] ConfigDefaults.php
DocType: php-stub-class
---
<?php
// Copyright 1999-2026. WebPros International GmbH. All rights reserved.
/**
 * Hook for extension config defaults (panel.ini settings)
 *
 * @package Plesk_Modules
 * @since 17.0
 */

query: retrieve extension configuration values
ground_truth: Extension configuration values are retrieved using pm_Config::get($name, $default), which returns the stored value for a given key.
reference_context:
[PHP-STUBS] Config.php
DocType: php-stub-class
---
class pm_Config
{

    /**
     * Retrieve extension's config parameter value by name
     *
     * @param string $name
     * @param mixed $default
     * @return mixed
     */
    public static function get($name, $default = null) { }

    /**
     * Retrieve extension's default configuration settings
     *
     * @return array
     * @since 18.0.59
     * */
    public static function getDefaults() { }

}

query: hook interface for Plesk modules
ground_truth: Plesk modules use a generic interface for hooks defined in Interface.php within the Plesk_Modules package.
reference_context:
[PHP-STUBS] Interface.php
DocType: php-stub-interface
---
<?php
// Copyright 1999-2026. WebPros International GmbH. All rights reserved.
/**
 * Generic interface for hooks
 *
 * @package Plesk_Modules
 * @since 12.0
 */

query: restart Plesk service from command line
ground_truth: Plesk services can be restarted from the CLI using 'plesk bin service --restart <service_name>'.
reference_context:
[CLI] service: Services
DocType: cli-command
---
Command: --restart or -r, Parameter: <service\_ name>, Description: Restarts a service., Example: To restart the DNS service: plesk bin service --restart dns or plesk bin service -r dns.

query: create a new subscription via CLI
ground_truth: Create a new subscription via CLI using the --create or -c command with plesk bin subscription.
reference_context:
[CLI] subscription: Subscriptions
DocType: cli-command
---
The --create or -c command, with the parameter <subscription\_name>, is used to create a new subscription.

query: list all domains via Plesk XML API
ground_truth: Domain information in Plesk is held by the admin-domain-list node in the XML API.
reference_context:
[API] List of Domains
DocType: api-reference
---
* Docs » * About XML API » * Reference » * Managing Plesk Server » * Getting Server Information » * Response Packet Structure and Samples » * List of Domains --- # List of Domains¶ The information about domains created in Plesk and belonging to the Plesk administrator is held by the **admin-domain-list** node.

query: authenticate with Plesk API using secret key
ground_truth: To authenticate with the REST API using a secret key, first create a key via /api/v2/auth/keys and then include it in the X-API-Key header.
reference_context:
[API] About REST API
DocType: api-reference
---
First, we create an API key to use it later for authentication: ``` curl -X POST --user root:password -H "Content-Type: application/json" "https://.../api/v2/auth/keys" ``` The key was created: ``` {"key":"..."} ``` Now we will use this API key... ``` curl -X GET -H "X-API-Key: ..." "https://.../api/v2/domains" ```

query: add a custom button to Plesk panel
ground_truth: Custom buttons are added by creating a class inherited from pm_Hook_CustomButtons in plib/hooks/.
reference_context:
[GUIDE] Add Custom Buttons
DocType: guide-topic
---
* Docs » * Plesk Features Available for Extensions » * Implement UI » * Integrate to Plesk UI » * Add Custom Buttons --- # Add Custom Buttons¶ To add custom buttons, you need to create a class inherited from pm\_Hook\_CustomButtons. The class must be placed in the directory `plib/hooks/`.

query: package a Plesk extension for distribution
ground_truth: Package an extension into a ZIP archive using: plesk bin extension --pack my_extension.
reference_context:
[GUIDE] How to Create and Install Extensions
DocType: guide-topic
---
Now you have to pack all the parts of extension into a ZIP archive ready for installation. Run the following command: ``` plesk bin extension --pack my_extension ```

query: register a new page in Plesk JS SDK
ground_truth: The Plesk Extension SDK offers routes to map URLs to components for creating extension pages.
reference_context:
[JS-SDK] 02-extension-sdk-features.md
DocType: js-sdk-guide
---
If you want to have all of these scenarios in your extension then you should create three different pages for it. To create pages, `plesk-ext-sdk` offers `routes`.

query: SSL certificate management
ground_truth: SSL/TLS certificate management in Plesk supports purchase, self-generation, and a two-level repository structure.
reference_context:
[API] Managing SSL/TLS Certificates
DocType: api-reference
---
* Docs » * About XML API » * Reference » * Managing SSL/TLS Certificates --- # Managing SSL/TLS Certificates¶ Using the Secure Sockets Layer protocol on a site requires an SSL/TLS certificate installed first. Plesk supports two-levels certificate repository.

query: backup and restore Plesk
ground_truth: Plesk provides commands for restoration from backup, including --restore, --check-backup, and --info.
reference_context:
[CLI] pleskrestore: Restoration from Backup
DocType: cli-command
---
``` plesk bin pleskrestore --restore "Backup-file" -only-databases ``` ## Commands¶ Command: --restore, Parameter: <backup\_file>, Description: Restores a backup file.

query: handle errors returned by the Plesk REST API
ground_truth: When XML API actions fail, Plesk returns a response packet with an errcode and errtext description.
reference_context:
[API] Error Codes
DocType: api-reference
---
When a request packet arrives at Plesk XML API server, it is validated... If any of these actions fail, Plesk sends back a response packet with an error code. For example: ``` <packet> ... <status>error</status> <errcode>1013</errcode> <errtext>Object not found.</errtext> </packet> ```

query: list all databases for a domain via API
ground_truth: The filter node is used in the XML API to specify criteria for selecting service plans or other database objects.
reference_context:
[API] Retrieving the List of Available Database Servers
DocType: api-reference
---
Its graphical representation is as follows: ![image-72627.png](_images/72627.png) * The **filter** node is *required*. It serves to specify the criteria by which the necessary service plans will be selected from the database.

query: enable or disable a Plesk extension from CLI
ground_truth: The plesk bin extension command supports various options like --pack and -no-ui for managing extensions via CLI.
reference_context:
[CLI] extension: Extensions
DocType: cli-command
---
For using with the --pack command., Example: To set the /tmp location for the exported file: plesk bin extension -p Mod\_1 -destination /tmp. Option: -no-ui, Description: Do not add resource files...

query: emit and listen to custom events in the Plesk JS SDK
ground_truth: The Plesk Extension SDK can be used alongside the UI Library, for example to use the Alert component in an extension.
reference_context:
[JS-SDK] 01-getting-started.md
DocType: js-sdk-guide
---
Let's see how we can use Plesk UI Library in an extension. We'll show how to use the [Alert](...) component from UI Library as an example.

query: what PHP version is required to use pm_Config
ground_truth: Plesk PHP stubs define parameters like PARAMETER_PHP_CGI_BIN, reflecting web-server configuration.

query: show DNS records for a domain via Plesk REST API
ground_truth: Plesk supports various DNS record types including A, AAAA, and NS.
reference_context:
[API] Adding DNS Record
DocType: api-reference
---
The following record types are available in Plesk: * *A* (Address). Used for storing an IPv4 32-bit address associated with a domain name. * *AAAA* (IPv6 address). * *NS* (Authoritative name server).

query: how to display a notification in the Plesk panel from an extension
ground_truth: The Plesk Extension SDK provides an Alert component to display information in the UI with various intents.

query: what happens if you call plesk bin subscription on a non-existent domain
ground_truth: Calling plesk bin subscription on a domain with the --update command allows modifying subscription options like DNS.
reference_context:
[CLI] subscription: Subscriptions
DocType: cli-command
---
The --update or -u command, with <subscription\_name> as a parameter, modifies settings for ## Options¶ This table details command-line options for managing Plesk subscriptions...
